libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Use APIs present in Pekko 2.0.0-M1. Do not reuse the old 1.1.5 TypedActor/TypedProps tests: Pekko 2.0 removes many deprecated APIs and the 2.0.0-M1 jar no longer contains org.apache.pekko.actor.TypedActor, TypedActorExtension, or TypedProps. Prefer classic actor APIs from pekko-actor itself, such as ActorSystem, Props, ActorRef, dispatchers, DynamicAccess, SerializationExtension, and ConfigFactory-backed configuration. Only retain the existing java.base/sun.net.dns add-exports if tests intentionally exercise DNS internals.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8742 [Automation] java run fails for org.apache.pekko:pekko-actor_2.13:2.0.0-M1; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The observed failure is caused by stale tests targeting removed Pekko 1.x TypedActor APIs, not by missing setup.

Pekko documentation lists pekko-testkit as a test dependency for conventional Pekko application tests, but it is not required to reach meaningful coverage of the pekko-actor artifact in this TCK scaffold.

Do not add separate Pekko modules such as pekko-actor-typed, pekko-remote, or persistence-related artifacts unless the generated tests intentionally switch scope to those artifacts; they are not required for the requested library.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency, Docker image, environment variable, or system property is required for meaningful coverage of org.apache.pekko:pekko-actor_2.13:2.0.0-M1; the resolved artifact only brings scala-library and com.typesafe:config, and normal usage is in-process ActorSystem/Props/serialization/configuration testing.
